speaker: POMPEY
text: I am as well acquainted here as I was in our

speaker: POMPEY
text: house of profession. One would think it were Mistress

speaker: POMPEY
text: Overdone’s own house, for here be many of

speaker: POMPEY
text: her old customers. First, here’s young Master Rash.

speaker: POMPEY
text: He’s in for a commodity of brown paper and old

speaker: POMPEY
text: ginger, ninescore and seventeen pounds, of which

speaker: POMPEY
text: he made five marks ready money. Marry, then

speaker: POMPEY
text: ginger was not much in request, for the old women

speaker: POMPEY
text: were all dead. Then is there here one Master Caper,

speaker: POMPEY
text: at the suit of Master Three-pile the mercer, for some

speaker: POMPEY
text: four suits of peach-colored satin, which now

speaker: POMPEY
text: peaches him a beggar. Then have we here young

speaker: POMPEY
text: Dizzy and young Master Deep-vow, and Master

speaker: POMPEY
text: Copper-spur and Master Starve-lackey the rapier-and-dagger

speaker: POMPEY
text: man, and young Drop-heir that killed

speaker: POMPEY
text: lusty Pudding, and Master Forth-light the tilter, and

speaker: POMPEY
text: brave Master Shoe-tie the great traveler, and wild

speaker: POMPEY
text: Half-can that stabbed Pots, and I think forty more,

speaker: POMPEY
text: all great doers in our trade, and are now “for the

speaker: POMPEY
text: Lord’s sake.”

speaker: ABHORSON
text: Sirrah, bring Barnardine hither.

speaker: POMPEY
text: Master Barnardine, you must rise

speaker: POMPEY
text: and be hanged, Master Barnardine.

speaker: ABHORSON
text: What ho, Barnardine!

speaker: BARNARDINE
text: A pox o’ your throats! Who makes

speaker: BARNARDINE
text: that noise there? What are you?

speaker: POMPEY
text: Your friends,

speaker: POMPEY
text: sir, the hangman. You must be so good, sir, to rise

speaker: POMPEY
text: and be put to death.

speaker: BARNARDINE
text: Away, you rogue, away! I am

speaker: BARNARDINE
text: sleepy.

speaker: ABHORSON
text: Tell him he must awake, and

speaker: ABHORSON
text: that quickly too.

speaker: POMPEY
text: Pray, Master Barnardine, awake till

speaker: POMPEY
text: you are executed, and sleep afterwards.

speaker: ABHORSON
text: Go in to him, and fetch him out.

speaker: POMPEY
text: He is coming, sir, he is coming. I hear his

speaker: POMPEY
text: straw rustle.

speaker: ABHORSON
text: Is the axe upon the block, sirrah?

speaker: POMPEY
text: Very ready, sir.

speaker: BARNARDINE
text: How now, Abhorson? What’s the news

speaker: BARNARDINE
text: with you?

speaker: ABHORSON
text: Truly, sir, I would desire you to clap into

speaker: ABHORSON
text: your prayers, for, look you, the warrant’s come.

speaker: BARNARDINE
text: You rogue, I have been drinking all night.

speaker: BARNARDINE
text: I am not fitted for ’t.

speaker: POMPEY
text: O, the better, sir, for he that drinks all night

speaker: POMPEY
text: and is hanged betimes in the morning may sleep the

speaker: POMPEY
text: sounder all the next day.

speaker: ABHORSON
text: Look you, sir, here comes

speaker: ABHORSON
text: your ghostly father. Do we jest now, think you?

speaker: DUKE
text: Sir, induced by my

speaker: DUKE
text: charity, and hearing how hastily you are to depart, I

speaker: DUKE
text: am come to advise you, comfort you, and pray with

speaker: DUKE
text: you.

speaker: BARNARDINE
text: Friar, not I. I have been drinking hard all

speaker: BARNARDINE
text: night, and I will have more time to prepare me, or

speaker: BARNARDINE
text: they shall beat out my brains with billets. I will not

speaker: BARNARDINE
text: consent to die this day, that’s certain.

speaker: DUKE
text: O, sir, you must. And therefore I

speaker: DUKE
text: beseech you look forward on the journey you shall

speaker: DUKE
text: go.

speaker: BARNARDINE
text: I swear I will not die today for any man’s

speaker: BARNARDINE
text: persuasion.

speaker: DUKE
text: But hear you—

speaker: BARNARDINE
text: Not a word. If you have anything to say to

speaker: BARNARDINE
text: me, come to my ward, for thence will not I today.

speaker: DUKE
text: Unfit to live or die. O gravel heart!

speaker: DUKE
text: After him, fellows; bring him to the block.

speaker: PROVOST
text: Now, sir, how do you find the prisoner?

speaker: DUKE
text: A creature unprepared, unmeet for death,

speaker: DUKE
text: And to transport him in the mind he is

speaker: DUKE
text: Were damnable.

speaker: PROVOST
text: Here in the prison, father,

speaker: PROVOST
text: There died this morning of a cruel fever

speaker: PROVOST
text: One Ragozine, a most notorious pirate,

speaker: PROVOST
text: A man of Claudio’s years, his beard and head

speaker: PROVOST
text: Just of his color. What if we do omit

speaker: PROVOST
text: This reprobate till he were well inclined,

speaker: PROVOST
text: And satisfy the Deputy with the visage

speaker: PROVOST
text: Of Ragozine, more like to Claudio?

speaker: DUKE
text: O, ’tis an accident that heaven provides!

speaker: DUKE
text: Dispatch it presently. The hour draws on

speaker: DUKE
text: Prefixed by Angelo. See this be done

speaker: DUKE
text: And sent according to command, whiles I

speaker: DUKE
text: Persuade this rude wretch willingly to die.

speaker: PROVOST
text: This shall be done, good father, presently.

speaker: PROVOST
text: But Barnardine must die this afternoon,

speaker: PROVOST
text: And how shall we continue Claudio,

speaker: PROVOST
text: To save me from the danger that might come

speaker: PROVOST
text: If he were known alive?

speaker: DUKE
text: Let this be done:

speaker: DUKE
text: Put them in secret holds, both Barnardine and Claudio.

speaker: DUKE
text: Ere twice the sun hath made his journal greeting

speaker: DUKE
text: To yonder generation, you shall find

speaker: DUKE
text: Your safety manifested.

speaker: PROVOST
text: I am your free dependent.

speaker: DUKE
text: Quick, dispatch, and send the head to Angelo.

speaker: DUKE
text: Now will I write letters to Angelo—

speaker: DUKE
text: The Provost he shall bear them—whose contents

speaker: DUKE
text: Shall witness to him I am near at home

speaker: DUKE
text: And that by great injunctions I am bound

speaker: DUKE
text: To enter publicly. Him I’ll desire

speaker: DUKE
text: To meet me at the consecrated fount

speaker: DUKE
text: A league below the city; and from thence,

speaker: DUKE
text: By cold gradation and well-balanced form,

speaker: DUKE
text: We shall proceed with Angelo.

speaker: PROVOST
text: Here is the head. I’ll carry it myself.

speaker: DUKE
text: Convenient is it. Make a swift return,

speaker: DUKE
text: For I would commune with you of such things

speaker: DUKE
text: That want no ear but yours.

speaker: PROVOST
text: I’ll make all speed.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Peace, ho, be here.

speaker: DUKE
text: The tongue of Isabel. She’s come to know

speaker: DUKE
text: If yet her brother’s pardon be come hither.

speaker: DUKE
text: But I will keep her ignorant of her good

speaker: DUKE
text: To make her heavenly comforts of despair

speaker: DUKE
text: When it is least expected.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Ho, by your leave.

speaker: DUKE
text: Good morning to you, fair and gracious daughter.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: The better, given me by so holy a man.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Hath yet the Deputy sent my brother’s pardon?

speaker: DUKE
text: He hath released him, Isabel, from the world.

speaker: DUKE
text: His head is off, and sent to Angelo.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Nay, but it is not so.

speaker: DUKE
text: It is no other.

speaker: DUKE
text: Show your wisdom, daughter, in your close patience.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: O, I will to him and pluck out his eyes!

speaker: DUKE
text: You shall not be admitted to his sight.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Unhappy Claudio, wretched Isabel,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Injurious world, most damnèd Angelo!

speaker: DUKE
text: This nor hurts him nor profits you a jot.

speaker: DUKE
text: Forbear it, therefore; give your cause to heaven.

speaker: DUKE
text: Mark what I say, which you shall find

speaker: DUKE
text: By every syllable a faithful verity.

speaker: DUKE
text: The Duke comes home tomorrow—nay, dry your eyes.

speaker: DUKE
text: One of our convent, and his confessor,

speaker: DUKE
text: Gives me this instance. Already he hath carried

speaker: DUKE
text: Notice to Escalus and Angelo,

speaker: DUKE
text: Who do prepare to meet him at the gates,

speaker: DUKE
text: There to give up their power. If you can, pace your wisdom

speaker: DUKE
text: In that good path that I would wish it go,

speaker: DUKE
text: And you shall have your bosom on this wretch,

speaker: DUKE
text: Grace of the Duke, revenges to your heart,

speaker: DUKE
text: And general honor.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: I am directed by you.

speaker: DUKE
text: This letter, then, to Friar Peter give.

speaker: DUKE
text: ’Tis that he sent me of the Duke’s return.

speaker: DUKE
text: Say, by this token, I desire his company

speaker: DUKE
text: At Mariana’s house tonight. Her cause and yours

speaker: DUKE
text: I’ll perfect him withal, and he shall bring you

speaker: DUKE
text: Before the Duke, and to the head of Angelo

speaker: DUKE
text: Accuse him home and home. For my poor self,

speaker: DUKE
text: I am combinèd by a sacred vow

speaker: DUKE
text: And shall be absent. Wend you with this letter.

speaker: DUKE
text: Command these fretting waters from your eyes

speaker: DUKE
text: With a light heart. Trust not my holy order

speaker: DUKE
text: If I pervert your course.—Who’s here?

speaker: LUCIO
text: Good even, friar, where’s the Provost?

speaker: DUKE
text: Not within, sir.

speaker: LUCIO
text: O, pretty Isabella, I am pale at mine heart to see

speaker: LUCIO
text: thine eyes so red. Thou must be patient. I am fain to

speaker: LUCIO
text: dine and sup with water and bran. I dare not for my

speaker: LUCIO
text: head fill my belly. One fruitful meal would set me to

speaker: LUCIO
text: ’t. But they say the Duke will be here tomorrow. By

speaker: LUCIO
text: my troth, Isabel, I loved thy brother. If the old

speaker: LUCIO
text: fantastical duke of dark corners had been at home,

speaker: LUCIO
text: he had lived.

speaker: DUKE
text: Sir, the Duke is marvelous little beholding

speaker: DUKE
text: to your reports, but the best is, he lives not

speaker: DUKE
text: in them.

speaker: LUCIO
text: Friar, thou knowest not the Duke so well as I do.

speaker: LUCIO
text: He’s a better woodman than thou tak’st him for.

speaker: DUKE
text: Well, you’ll answer this one day. Fare

speaker: DUKE
text: you well.

speaker: LUCIO
text: Nay, tarry, I’ll go along with thee. I can tell thee

speaker: LUCIO
text: pretty tales of the Duke.

speaker: DUKE
text: You have told me too many of him

speaker: DUKE
text: already, sir, if they be true; if not true, none were

speaker: DUKE
text: enough.

speaker: LUCIO
text: I was once before him for getting a wench with

speaker: LUCIO
text: child.

speaker: DUKE
text: Did you such a thing?

speaker: LUCIO
text: Yes, marry, did I, but I was fain to forswear it.

speaker: LUCIO
text: They would else have married me to the rotten

speaker: LUCIO
text: medlar.

speaker: DUKE
text: Sir, your company is fairer than honest.

speaker: DUKE
text: Rest you well.

speaker: LUCIO
text: By my troth, I’ll go with thee to the lane’s end. If

speaker: LUCIO
text: bawdy talk offend you, we’ll have very little of it.

speaker: LUCIO
text: Nay, friar, I am a kind of burr. I shall stick.
